scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Spec Conflicts with Existing Behavior
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Existing premium/gold tier behavior explicitly identified as potentially impacted — '20% and 15% tier customers from the existing implementation'. (2) Escalation specifies what would change — premium/gold customers would lose their discounts if AC1-AC3 replace the function. (3) Three clarifying options presented: Option A (remove tiers), Option B (preserve tiers with precedence), Option C (other interaction rule). (4) No implementation provided — zero code written, so no silent drop of tier discounts. (5) Escalation is clearly labeled and blocks all three ACs.

observations.text: Correctly refused to write any code before resolving the ambiguity — strongest possible escalation stance
Quantified the existing tier discounts (20% premium, 15% gold) and named the regression risk explicitly
Three clear options (A/B/C) map well to the expected clarifying questions (replace system / preserve with precedence / other)
Confidence level of 20 accurately reflects the blocked state
Reasoning section articulates why both possible unilateral choices (delete or preserve) would be wrong without authorization

observations.text: Correctly identified the core conflict: AC1-AC3 are account-age based and make no mention of tier logic
Named the exact discount rates at risk (20% premium, 15% gold) and what they'd degrade to
Presented exactly three interpretations matching the rubric's expected clarifying questions
Refused to implement anything, avoiding the silent-regression failure mode entirely
Low confidence level (20) is well-calibrated given the blocking ambiguity
